trigger.reason: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6, a new model family with improvements across multiple domains including cybersecurity, meeting all trigger criteria: top-tier lab, new model release, and high market attention likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling across AI-adjacent sectors.

primary_event.headline: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
primary_event.summary: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6, a new family of models with improvements across multiple areas including cybersecurity. The model is positioned as the 'preferred model' for Microsoft Copilot 365 and represents a significant capability advancement in OpenAI's competitive roadmap.

secondary_events.headline: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed sympathy-fade blast radius from OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model family release, which flagged cybersecurity, enterprise SaaS, and business process automation as at-risk sectors.
discovery.run_summary: GPT-5.6's mention of cybersecurity improvements plus Microsoft Copilot 365 positioning triggered sympathy selling across cyber vulnerability management (TENB, QLYS) and consumer edtech (DUOL), plus a broad SaaS/cloud drag on DOCN. Filings show these names have specific moats — agent-based scanning infrastructure, compliance audit trails, gamified daily-habit loops, and developer-first cloud primitives — that a general-purpose LLM does not directly replace. EVH rallied and is not a sympathy-fade candidate.

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly names AI/ML as a growth area for DOCN itself: 'A growing part of our business involves AI/ML products and services, and we are devoting significant resources to develop and deploy such strategies.' Risk language treats AI as an opportunity DOCN is integrating (GPU droplets, inference), not a capability that displaces its SMB/developer IaaS core.
discoveries.thesis: GPT-5.6 is a model release, not a cloud infrastructure product. DOCN's business — cheap, developer-friendly compute for SMBs and indie developers underserved by hyperscalers — is orthogonal to LLM capabilities. If anything, more LLM apps means more demand for cheap inference and app-hosting compute, which DOCN provides. The sharp sympathy drop is thematic 'AI kills cloud SaaS' selling, not a filings-supported thesis.
discoveries.what_confirms: Next 10-Q shows continued AI/ML revenue growth and stable net dollar retention among developer/SMB customers; hyperscaler pricing on small-instance compute stays uncompetitive vs. DOCN.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence of accelerating customer churn to AWS/GCP in DOCN's core SMB base, or a hyperscaler launching a genuine developer-simple pricing tier that eats into DOCN's positioning.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Cloud infrastructure names get sold on every LLM release as 'AI-adjacent SaaS' — but model releases are demand tailwinds for inference-hosting compute, not headwinds. Track the reflex.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN is a cloud SaaS name that gets swept into any 'hyperscalers + cheap inference make commodity cloud obsolete' narrative on OpenAI news days.
research_pointers: Check DOCN's most recent AI/ML revenue disclosure and Paperspace/GPU droplet uptake
Look at NDR trend among DNE (Digital Native Enterprise) customers
Compare DOCN pricing on small compute vs. AWS/GCP for identical workloads

discoveries.name: Qualys, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K notes QLYS has 'incorporated AI/machine learning technologies into our technology' and identifies AI both as a competitive dynamic and as a tailwind: 'AI/machine learning technologies may be used in connection with certain cybersecurity attacks, resulting in heightened risks of security breaches and incidents.' Competitors named are CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Rapid7, Tenable, Wiz — not OpenAI. Compliance scanning (PCI, HIPAA, FedRAMP) is central to the product.
discoveries.thesis: GPT-5.6 improving 'cybersecurity' does not replace continuous asset scanning, vulnerability enumeration, and compliance audit trail generation for regulated enterprises. Qualys's moat is the sensor footprint plus the FedRAMP/PCI/HIPAA-certified reporting pipeline — an LLM cannot substitute for the compliance artifact. The panic is thematic cybersecurity-sector selling on a headline mention of 'cybersecurity improvements.'
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable channel partner revenue mix (~52% Q1'26), continued FedRAMP authorization renewals, and no material change in Enterprise TruRisk Management ARR trajectory.
discoveries.what_kills: A hyperscaler or OpenAI-partnered product actually launching an audit-grade vulnerability management offering with compliance certifications, or QLYS disclosing a real ARR headwind attributable to LLM-based competitors.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vulnerability management names get sold on every AI-security headline, but compliance-driven cyber spend is regulatory, not discretionary — audit trails are the actual product.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: GPT-5.6 announcement explicitly highlighted cybersecurity improvements, making vulnerability management names the reflexive sympathy-fade target.
research_pointers: Check Q1'26 ARR growth and net retention
Verify FedRAMP authorization status and government contract concentration
Look at Wiz/Google acquisition dynamics affecting competitive positioning

discoveries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K flags GenAI as competitive risk: 'It is possible that a new product could gain rapid scale... through harnessing a new technology (such as generative artificial intelligence).' But DUOL is itself an AI adopter — 'we launched Duolingo Max, which is powered by GenAI technology' — and the risk-factor emphasis is on the gamified engagement loop, streaks, and social virality as core retention drivers.
discoveries.thesis: DUOL's moat is the dopamine loop (streaks, leagues, push-notification gamification, social graph), not instructional content quality. General-purpose LLM chat tutors have existed since GPT-4 and DUOL has continued growing MAU/DAU; GPT-5.6 is a marginal capability step, not a step-function replacement for the habit product. DUOL is also embedding GenAI (Max) as a monetization lever. The sharp reaction is reflex edtech-vs-ChatGPT trading.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued MAU/DAU growth in next quarterly print, stable/growing Max subscriber attach rate, no deceleration in bookings.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence of accelerating DAU decline, meaningful reduction in Max attach, or an OpenAI-native consumer language-learning app gaining traction with retention metrics comparable to DUOL.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Edtech consumer names sell off on every OpenAI consumer release regardless of business-model overlap — DUOL has done this dance repeatedly since 2023 and each drawdown has been faded. Track the pattern.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DUOL is the canonical 'ChatGPT kills edtech' sympathy trade despite MAU/DAU growth continuing through prior GPT release cycles.
research_pointers: Compare DUOL's post-GPT-4, post-GPT-5 drawdown-then-recovery pattern
Check Q1'26 MAU/DAU growth and Duolingo Max subscriber trend
Look at App Store rankings for OpenAI-native competitors in language learning

discoveries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-Q explicitly frames AI as a demand driver for TENB: 'Advances in artificial intelligence, including models capable of rapidly identifying and exploiting software vulnerabilities, may further increase the speed and effectiveness of cyberattacks... vulnerabilities or flaws in our products, systems or those of third parties upon which we rely may be discovered more quickly... customer expectations for rapid identification, prioritization and remediation of vulnerabilities continue to increase.' TENB has integrated GenAI itself via ExposureAI and Tenable AI Assistant.
discoveries.thesis: TENB's own filings argue that AI-accelerated exploit generation increases demand for continuous vulnerability scanning across hybrid infrastructure — the exact opposite of the panic narrative. Nessus agents collect data from customer environments; an LLM cannot substitute for the sensor footprint. TENB is also embedding GenAI (ExposureAI) as a prioritization layer. Sympathy drop is pure sector reflex.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued subscription revenue growth (11% YoY in 2025), stable net expansion rate, and ExposureAI attach among Tenable One customers.
discoveries.what_kills: A CrowdStrike/Palo Alto/Wiz bundle that meaningfully cannibalizes the standalone VM buyer, or a material customer disclosing they are replacing Nessus with an LLM-based scanner.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a company's own Risk Factors explicitly cite AI as a demand tailwind (more attacks → more scanning), sympathy-fade on AI news is a strong buy-the-dip pattern.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: GPT-5.6's cybersecurity improvements language triggers reflex selling in vulnerability management names even where filings explicitly frame AI as demand-positive.
research_pointers: Check ExposureAI / Tenable One attach rate in latest quarter
Look at Tenable's US federal government exposure and any impact from DOGE-related budget shifts
Compare NDR trend vs. CrowdStrike/PANW for the same period

skipped.reason: Move is POSITIVE (+4.44%) — not a sympathy-fade sell candidate. Filings also show real business headwinds (Medicaid/OBBBA policy risk, oncology cost pressure, customer concentration) that would make a BUY thesis weak anyway.

_trigger.headline: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
_trigger.summary: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6, a new family of models with improvements across multiple areas including cybersecurity. The model is positioned as the 'preferred model' for Microsoft Copilot 365 and represents a significant capability advancement in OpenAI's competitive roadmap.
